title: Happy Number
problem_statement: Determine whether repeatedly replacing a number with the sum of the squares of its digits eventually reaches 1.

examples.explanation: 19 -> 82 -> 68 -> 100 -> 1, so it is a happy number.

intuition: Use cycle detection to identify if the sequence reaches 1 or loops indefinitely.

approaches.name: Fast and slow pointers
approaches.description: Compute the next number repeatedly with two pointers; if they meet at 1 return true, otherwise detect a loop.
approaches.why_not: A hash set also detects cycles but uses extra memory.

interview_qa.a: To detect a cycle in the transformation sequence without extra space.
The sequence reaches 1 before a cycle is detected.
